label: Cold Start
note: Deck only. Essentially zero public signal, so there is nothing to score and the gate must route to PROOF_PROTOCOL rather than NO_CALL. Deck claims are deliberately technically specific: each one is falsifiable by a challenge artifact.

profiles.one_liner: Cross-region vector index with single-digit-millisecond p99 on commodity NVMe.
profiles.absence_rationale: Infrastructure built inside a prior employer's walls produces no public artifact. Absence here is expected, not evasive.

payload.claim: p99 read latency under 900 microseconds for a 1.2B-vector index sharded across three regions, on commodity NVMe, no GPU in the serving path

events.evidence_span: slide 3: "p99 < 900us @ 1.2B vectors, 3 regions, commodity NVMe, zero GPU in the read path"

payload.claim: index rebuild is incremental: a 1% document churn costs 1% of a full rebuild, not a full reindex

events.evidence_span: slide 4: "incremental rebuild is linear in churn, not in corpus size"

payload.claim: recall@10 holds above 0.94 against exact search while reading at most 0.3% of the index per query

events.evidence_span: slide 5: "recall@10 >= 0.94 vs brute force, touching <=0.3% of index bytes per query"

payload.claim: a single region can be lost mid-query and the read completes from surviving replicas without a retry storm

events.evidence_span: slide 6: "region-loss during read is transparent; no client retry, no fan-out amplification"

payload.fact: solo technical founder, five years building storage systems, none of it public
payload.affiliation_note: prior affiliation recorded as a neutral biographical fact and excluded from every scoring input

events.evidence_span: slide 8: "Five years on distributed storage. All of it behind an NDA, none of it on GitHub."

profiles.absence_rationale: Two founders, no repos, no talks, no papers. Nothing to score either way.

payload.claim: bit-identical replay of a multi-stream CUDA workload, including race-dependent kernels, at under 3% wall-clock overhead

events.evidence_span: slide 2: "bit-identical replay of racing multi-stream kernels, <3% overhead"

payload.claim: recording requires no kernel source changes and no recompilation; it intercepts at the driver API

events.evidence_span: slide 3: "driver-API interposition. No source, no recompile, no vendor toolchain fork."

payload.claim: trace size stays under 40MB per minute for a full training step loop

events.evidence_span: slide 4: "<40MB/min trace for a full step loop"

payload.fact: two founders, no public code, no conference talks
payload.affiliation_note: neutral biographical fact only; not a scoring input

events.evidence_span: slide 9: "We have never open-sourced anything. That is a fair thing to hold against us."
